scenarios.description: charset(html syntax, updateMetaCharsetElement false) sets outputSettings charset only without touching head
scenarios.expectedResult: outputSettings charset updated, no <meta charset> or removal of obsolete <meta[name=charset]>
pseudocode: TEST TC06 charset with updateMetaCharsetElement false

scenarios.description: charset(xml syntax, existing XmlDeclaration with non-xml name) prepends a new xml declaration and leaves the old one intact
scenarios.path: B0→B1(ensureMetaCharset true)→B3(syntax xml)→B4(node instanceof XmlDeclaration && name != xml)→B5
scenarios.expectedResult: two XmlDeclaration nodes: new one first with encoding and version, old 'foo' remains second
pseudocode: TEST TC07 xml syntax existing non-xml declaration

scenarios.description: charset(xml syntax, existing XmlDeclaration named 'xml' with version) updates only encoding and resets version to '1.0'
scenarios.path: B0→B1(ensureMetaCharset true)→B3(syntax xml)→B4(node instanceof XmlDeclaration && name == xml && has version)→B6
scenarios.precondition: doc.outputSettings().syntax = xml; updateMetaCharsetElement(true); prependChild(XmlDeclaration("xml",false) with version="2.0" and encoding="OLD")
scenarios.expectedResult: existing XmlDeclaration updated: encoding set to new charset, version reset to '1.0'
pseudocode: TEST TC08 xml syntax existing xml declaration with version
